prompt: String.split produces unexpected results. What is the bug?
choices: split only works with single-character delimiters
In regex, '.' matches any character; split(".") splits on every character, producing an empty array; to split on a literal dot use split("\\.") or split(Pattern.quote("."))
split returns null when the pattern matches the entire string
split("." ) splits on whitespace by default
explanation: split takes a regex. '.' is a regex metacharacter matching any character. split(".") on "a.b.c" matches every position, and Java's split discards trailing empty strings, yielding an empty array (length 0). Fix: split("\\.") to match a literal dot.
